title: Mountain Of Madness

jokes.text: No. The promise of a new day is more than enough exhilaration for me.
jokes.setup: Smithers offers coffee to start the day
jokes.punchline: Burns finds the mere existence of morning intoxicating enough
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization - his detachment from normal human experience delivered with aristocratic precision

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Will you be having your usual morning coffee, sir?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: No, thank you, Smithers. I find the mere existence of morning intoxicating enough.

jokes.text: Chinese checkers or domestic, sir?
jokes.setup: Burns asks for coffee, then says he wants excitement instead
jokes.punchline: Smithers mishears 'excitement' as coffee types
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Smithers' eagerness to please even when confused

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Smithers, bring me some coffee.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Right away, sir.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: On second thought, forget the coffee. I want excitement!

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Very good, sir. Will that be a Maxwell House or a Folgers?

jokes.text: All right. Popcorn's ready.
jokes.setup: Burns decides to conduct a fire drill
jokes.punchline: He's prepared popcorn to watch his employees panic
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Burns' sadistic enjoyment of his workers' suffering with economic precision

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Smithers, prepare the popcorn. I'm conducting a fire drill.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Very good, sir. Shall I alert the employees?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: No, no. That would spoil the fun. I want to see genuine panic.

jokes.text: Fire, fire, fire, fire, fire, fire, fire, fire!
jokes.setup: Fire alarm is going off, everyone is evacuating
jokes.punchline: Homer mindlessly chants 'fire' while running
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer panic behavior - repetitive and simple minded

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Fire! Fire! Fire! Fire!

jokes.text: This stopwatch only goes up to 15 minutes.
jokes.setup: Burns asks for evacuation time, expecting 45 seconds
jokes.punchline: It's taken so long they've exceeded the stopwatch capacity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of incompetence - the evacuation is hilariously slow

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: How long until everyone is evacuated?

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Well sir, at the current rate, it should take approximately 45 seconds.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: 45 seconds?! That's an eternity!

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Actually sir, it's taken so long we've exceeded the stopwatch capacity.

jokes.text: I think I won, Mr. Burns.
jokes.setup: Terrible evacuation performance, everyone else stuck behind door
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks being first out means he 'won'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness to the actual situation and his role in it

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: In your face, everyone! I won! I'm first out!

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Homer, we're trapped behind this door!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's your problem now!

jokes.text: I've seen more orderly behavior in a Ritz Brothers film.
jokes.setup: Burns criticizing the chaotic evacuation
jokes.punchline: References obscure 1930s slapstick comedy team
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns reference - shows his age and cultural touchstones while insulting employees

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: This evacuation is a complete disaster! It's like watching the Mumblebee Brothers try to defuse a bomb!

jokes.text: Hit him, Carl! You too, Lenny!
jokes.setup: Workers arguing about teamwork
jokes.punchline: Bart enthusiastically encourages violence
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart mischief-making, though not particularly original

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Come on guys, we gotta work together as a team! That means no fighting!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Now let's go out there and kick some ass!

jokes.text: which means we'll have to cancel our plans to hang around here.
jokes.setup: Corporate retreat will disrupt their weekend
jokes.punchline: Their 'plans' were just hanging around
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of how mundane their lives are

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, the corporate retreat is this weekend. I thought we had plans.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Plans? Marge, our plans were just hanging around.

jokes.text: Think about it. I mean, what team was Babe Ruth on? Who knows?
jokes.setup: Homer argues against teamwork using Babe Ruth
jokes.punchline: He doesn't know what team Ruth played for
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - using an example that undermines his own argument through ignorance

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Babe Ruth didn't need a team. He was a lone wolf.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, Babe Ruth played for the Yankees.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: The Yankees? What team is that?

jokes.text: And what's all this crap I've been hearing about tolerance?
jokes.setup: Homer listing things he's against
jokes.punchline: Tolerance presented as obviously bad thing
jokes.explanation: Homer's backwards values played for satirical effect

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm against things. Tolerance, equality, progress...

jokes.text: Your ideas are intriguing to me and I wish to subscribe to your newsletter.
jokes.setup: Homer's anti-social rant
jokes.punchline: Formal, polite request to subscribe to newsletter about terrible ideas
jokes.explanation: Became iconic meme - perfect contrast between formal politeness and enthusiasm for Homer's awful philosophy

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: I don't want to make friends, I don't want to learn about current events, I don't want to help the poor, and I don't want to work for a democracy.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown worker
dialogue.line: Well, would you at least consider subscribing to our newsletter about terrible ideas?

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Now you're speaking my language!

jokes.text: Budget cutbacks have forced us to eliminate anything the least bit entertaining.
jokes.setup: Marge says they can have fun at the national park
jokes.punchline: Government bureaucracy has eliminated all fun
jokes.explanation: Good satirical jab at government bureaucracy and budget cuts

dialogue.speaker: Park Ranger
dialogue.line: Welcome to the national park! I'm afraid we've had to eliminate all the fun. Government bureaucracy, you understand. We used to have a seesaw, but we removed it because someone might fall off. We had a merry-go-round, but it was too merry. And the swings? Too swinging. So now we just have a quiet place to stand and think about the rules.

jokes.text: Uh, I lost my map. You haven't been issued a map yet.
jokes.setup: Homer claims he lost his map
jokes.punchline: Burns points out maps haven't been given out yet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer illogic - losing something he never had

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I lost my map.

dialogue.speaker: Burns
dialogue.line: But Homer, we haven't given out the maps yet.

jokes.text: Hey, this sounds like fun. And the last team to arrive will be fired. [Chuckles] Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about the competition
jokes.punchline: Delayed realization of the serious consequences
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer delayed reaction beat - enthusiasm turning to panic

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Woo-hoo! A competition! I'm gonna win so much money!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait... what are the stakes again?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh... oh no. Oh no no no no no.

jokes.text: Not bloody likely.
jokes.setup: Burns suggests he might be the one who gets fired
jokes.punchline: Smithers' quiet, sarcastic response to obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Good Smithers cynicism about Burns' false modesty

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Yes, sir. And perhaps I'm the Queen of England.

jokes.text: Aw, nuts! I mean, um- Aw, nuts!
jokes.setup: Lenny paired with Carl
jokes.punchline: Tries to be diplomatic but gives up
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment - Lenny's failed attempt at politeness

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Look, Carl, I'm trying to be diplomatic here, but you know what? Forget it.

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: What? What were you gonna say?

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Nah, I'm done trying to be nice about this.

jokes.text: Quiet, you fool! You're on the one team that can't possibly be fired.
jokes.setup: Homer initially upset about being paired with Burns
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's actually in the safest position
jokes.explanation: Homer's rare moment of workplace political awareness

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... Mr. Burns is here with me, which means he's not out there causing trouble. And all his money is just sitting in the bank doing nothing. I'm in the safest position in Springfield!

jokes.text: Yes. Well, frankly, you've been a bit of a pill lately.
jokes.setup: Smithers discovers Burns didn't rig the drawing in his favor
jokes.punchline: Burns admits to deliberately excluding his loyal assistant
jokes.explanation: Burns' casual cruelty to his most devoted employee

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Yes, well, I deliberately excluded you from the drawing, Smithers.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: But sir, why would you do that? I've been loyal to you for decades!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Precisely, Smithers. That's exactly why. A man of your devotion deserves better than to win a drawing. You deserve to suffer like the common rabble.

jokes.text: Perfect. That's just perfect!
jokes.setup: Smithers realizes he's paired with himself (last name)
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic reaction to being essentially abandoned
jokes.explanation: Smithers' justified sarcasm at his predicament

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Oh, how delightful. I'm paired with myself. Well, at least I know I'll be in good hands. Or should I say, my own hands? How romantic.

jokes.text: You pressed 'You,' referring to me. That is incorrect. The correct answer is you.
jokes.setup: Educational machine about forest fires
jokes.punchline: Confusing circular logic about 'you'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay about pronoun confusion in educational materials

dialogue.speaker: Smokey Bear machine
dialogue.line: Only you can prevent forest fires.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Me? But I'm not a bear!

dialogue.speaker: Smokey Bear machine
dialogue.line: Only YOU can prevent forest fires.

jokes.text: Mom, can Lisa and I play outside away from the bear?
jokes.setup: Family stuck at ranger station
jokes.punchline: Casual request to avoid dangerous wildlife
jokes.explanation: Good understated delivery of genuinely dangerous situation

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why can't we just go outside?

dialogue.speaker: Ranger
dialogue.line: Because of the bears, sir.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: So just don't go near the bears.

dialogue.speaker: Ranger
dialogue.line: It's not that simple. They're everywhere.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, can't you just ask them to leave?

jokes.text: Bony old behind! Bony old behind! Why, thank you, Simpson. I have been watching my figure.
jokes.setup: Echo repeats Smithers insulting Burns' behind
jokes.punchline: Burns takes it as a compliment about his fitness
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Burns' vanity making him interpret insult as compliment

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Smithers, that's the nicest thing you've ever said to me.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: But sir, I didn't say anything.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: The echo, you fool! It said my posterior was impressive.

jokes.text: Lise! Lise! Come here! I found two snowflakes that are exactly alike!
jokes.setup: Bart calls Lisa over excitedly
jokes.punchline: Claims impossible scientific discovery
jokes.explanation: Good setup for physical gag, plays on common knowledge about snowflake uniqueness

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Lisa! Lisa! Come quick!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: What is it, Bart?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I've discovered a new element! It's heavier than lead, lighter than air, and it glows in the dark!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, that's physically impossible.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Exactly! That's why no one else has found it yet!

jokes.text: Oh, great. It's the Bobbsey Twins.
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa approach Smithers
jokes.punchline: References old children's book series
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate reference showing Smithers' cultural knowledge and irritation

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Hey Smithers, what are you reading?

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Oh, just the classic 'Nancy Drew' series. I've been collecting them since I was a boy.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Nancy Drew? I thought you were more into... other types of books.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Well yes, but a gentleman must have refined literary tastes. Besides, Nancy is quite resourceful and independent.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yeah, right. We all know you're just into the mystery solving part.

jokes.text: 12:80. No, wait. Wait. What comes after 12? One. No. After 12.
jokes.setup: Smithers asks Bart for the time
jokes.punchline: Bart fundamentally doesn't understand how clocks work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart confusion - completely wrong about basic concepts

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: What time is it?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Uh, it's... the little hand is on the big number and the big hand is on the little number.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: That doesn't help at all.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yeah, well clocks are stupid.

jokes.text: Why should the race always be to the swift? Or the Jumble to the quick-witted?
jokes.setup: Burns philosophizing about cheating
jokes.punchline: Compares life competition to newspaper word puzzle
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns philosophy - mixing biblical reference with mundane puzzle game

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Life is like a newspaper crossword puzzle, you know. Sometimes you lie a little to make things fit.

jokes.text: Well, I say cheating is a gift man gives himself.
jokes.setup: Burns' philosophical justification for cheating
jokes.punchline: Presents dishonesty as noble self-actualization
jokes.explanation: Peak Burns philosophy - perfectly crafted inversion of morality delivered with conviction

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: You see, Simpson, dishonesty is not a vice—it's a virtue. It's the pursuit of self-actualization in its purest form. When a man lies, cheats, and steals his way to the top, he's not committing crimes—he's engaging in noble self-improvement. The weak call it fraud. I call it philosophy.

jokes.text: I propose we travel by... horseless sleigh.
jokes.setup: Burns suggests they use vehicle to cheat
jokes.punchline: Calls snowmobile by archaic term
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns vocabulary - shows his disconnect from modern world

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: We'll use the snowmobile to cheat!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Quickly, to the sleigh!

jokes.text: You know, Simpson, you're not as objectionable as you seemed when we first met. No, sir, I am not.
jokes.setup: Burns gives Homer a backhanded compliment
jokes.punchline: Homer enthusiastically agrees with the insult
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness to being insulted while being complimented

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: You're not as stupid as you look, Simpson.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Thanks, Mr. Burns!

jokes.text: Follow me. We'll take the chairlift. It'll give us an eagle-eye view of the area directly beneath the chairlift.
jokes.setup: Ranger offering to help find children
jokes.punchline: Chairlift only covers tiny search area
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan humor about the limitations of their search method

dialogue.speaker: Park Ranger
dialogue.line: Don't worry, I'll help you find your children. We can search the entire forest using the chairlift.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Great!

dialogue.speaker: Park Ranger
dialogue.line: Of course, the chairlift only covers a tiny search area.

jokes.text: Well, I won't lie to you, ma'am. Our chances of finding your children are slim to nil.
jokes.setup: Marge asks for help finding lost children
jokes.punchline: Ranger immediately declares mission hopeless
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about incompetent park service

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Can you help us find our children? They're lost in the woods.

dialogue.speaker: Park Ranger
dialogue.line: Lost in the woods? They're probably dead. Most likely they've been eaten by a bear or a wolf. At best, they're freezing to death right now. Mission's hopeless.

jokes.text: There they are! Let me down here. Sorry. There's no way off till we get to the top.
jokes.setup: They immediately spot the children from chairlift
jokes.punchline: Can't get off to help them despite seeing them
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - finding kids but being unable to reach them

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh my God, the children!

dialogue.speaker: Ranger
dialogue.line: We can't stop the chairlift!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: We have to do something!

dialogue.speaker: Ranger
dialogue.line: There's nothing we can do. We're stuck up here.

jokes.text: Um, do you know how to weld?
jokes.setup: Getting off chairlift is 'sort of tricky'
jokes.punchline: Requires welding skills
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of how poorly maintained the park equipment is

dialogue.speaker: Park Ranger
dialogue.line: Getting off the chairlift is sort of tricky. It requires welding skills.

jokes.text: No going through the window for us.
jokes.setup: Homer and Burns arriving at cabin in style
jokes.punchline: Homer proud of using door like normal person
jokes.explanation: Homer's pride in basic civilized behavior

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mr. Burns, look! I opened the door like a normal person!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Simpson, you used the door handle instead of having Smithers oil the hinges and push it open with his face.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: See? I'm learning!

jokes.text: There isn't any food in any of these trees.
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa searching for food
jokes.punchline: Lisa states obvious with scientific precision
jokes.explanation: Lisa's rational response to Bart's illogical food search

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Lisa, I'm starving. We need to find food.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: According to my calculations, the human body can only survive approximately 3 weeks without food. We have roughly 20 days and 14 hours before organ failure becomes inevitable.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Lisa, that doesn't help us RIGHT NOW.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Well, statistically speaking, we're still alive.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm not gonna get fired. Is there any gold in this mountain?
jokes.setup: Smithers worried about being fired
jokes.punchline: Bart completely ignores concern, asks about gold
jokes.explanation: Bart's self-centered response and get-rich-quick thinking

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: I'm worried Mr. Burns might fire me.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dude, that's terrible. By the way, is that gold?

jokes.text: Mr. Smithers! Mr Smithers! I found another hurt shrew. I think this one has a twisted ankle.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to help Smithers
jokes.punchline: Her focus on tiny injured animals during emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - her environmental concern even in crisis

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Mr. Smithers, I'm here to help! What's the emergency?

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Oh thank goodness, Lisa. I've been hit by a car and I think my leg is broken.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Don't worry, I'll call an ambulance right away... Oh my God, there's a injured field mouse over there! And look, a baby sparrow!

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Lisa, I'm still bleeding!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Just hold on, Mr. Smithers. These creatures need me more. This moth's wing is slightly bent.

jokes.text: Way richer than Lenny.
jokes.setup: Homer acknowledging Burns is rich
jokes.punchline: Compares to obviously poor coworker
jokes.explanation: Homer's working-class perspective on wealth disparity

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mr. Burns is rich. He's got more money than Barney.

jokes.text: Oh, yes, sitting. The great leveler. From the mightiest pharaoh to the lowliest peasant... who doesn't enjoy a good sit?
jokes.setup: Homer comments on comfortable chairs
jokes.punchline: Burns philosophizes about sitting as universal human experience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns pomposity - treating mundane action as profound universal truth

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Wow, this is one comfortable chair!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Yes, well, sitting is a universal human experience. Whether you're a king or a pauper, a CEO or a safety inspector, we all must sit. It's what separates us from the animals.

jokes.text: Why get up? Here's a little move I've been tinkering with.
jokes.setup: Burns mentions difficulty of getting up
jokes.punchline: Homer presents laziness as innovation
jokes.explanation: Homer treating sloth as ingenuity - leads to physical comedy

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: I'm having difficulty getting up in the morning.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You know, Mr. Burns, that's not a problem. That's an opportunity. What you need is the Homer Simpson Alarm Clock System.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Step one: Don't set an alarm. Step two: Sleep as late as you want. Step three: Wake up whenever your body tells you to.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: It's called laziness, but I like to think of it as... efficiency.

jokes.text: Sir, I am in your debt.
jokes.setup: Homer shows Burns how to reach things without getting up
jokes.punchline: Burns treats laziness technique as profound wisdom
jokes.explanation: Burns' formal gratitude for learning sloth techniques

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mr. Burns, instead of getting up, just use this back scratcher to reach things.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Remarkable! You've given me a tool that extends my reach without exertion. You're a genius!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, I wouldn't say genius...

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: From this day forward, I shall apply this philosophy to all aspects of my life. Why walk when one can be carried? Why think when one can have others think for you? Simpson, you've revolutionized my existence!

jokes.text: Sir, I am in your debt. / Use it wisely, my friend.
jokes.setup: Burns impressed by Homer's lazy technique
jokes.punchline: Homer's mock-serious response to gratitude
jokes.explanation: Homer treating his laziness technique like ancient wisdom

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Simpson, I'm impressed by your lazy technique. You've mastered the art of doing nothing.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, thank you, sir. I've been practicing since I was a boy.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: It's a rare talent.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I know. My father would be proud... if he cared.

jokes.text: Hey, did you hear something? No. Hmm. Did I? I don't know!
jokes.setup: Homer asking about avalanche noise
jokes.punchline: Confuses himself about what he heard
jokes.explanation: Homer's confusion escalating into philosophical uncertainty about his own senses

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: What was that noise? Was that an avalanche?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: No, wait... that was me. I'm an avalanche.

jokes.text: Like, maybe the cabin is the place inside each of us... created by our goodwill and teamwork. Nah. They said there'd be sandwiches.
jokes.setup: Carl suggests cabin might be metaphorical
jokes.punchline: Lenny rejects philosophy for practical food concern
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of pseudo-philosophical teamwork rhetoric with practical working-class concerns

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Maybe the cabin is metaphorical, you know? Like, a state of mind.

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Carl, I don't care about metaphors. I'm hungry.

jokes.text: I'm not quite the tunnel-digger I made myself out to be.
jokes.setup: Burns and Homer need to dig out
jokes.punchline: Burns admits he lied about manual labor skills
jokes.explanation: Burns admitting obvious truth about his lack of physical capability

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Release the hounds! We'll dig our way out!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But sir, I thought you said you had manual labor skills.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: I lied. I've never done a day's work in my life.

jokes.text: Burns and Associate. No, no! What am I thinking? Burns and Teammate.
jokes.setup: Burns imagining their partnership name
jokes.punchline: Corrects himself to slightly less hierarchical title
jokes.explanation: Burns' minimal concession to equality while still making himself the senior partner

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: The partnership shall be called 'Burns and Smithers'.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Or rather, 'Burns, Burns, and Smithers'.

jokes.text: Those last three avalanches were your fault, Simpson. So what? Sew buttons!
jokes.setup: Burns blames Homer for avalanches
jokes.punchline: Homer's nonsensical defiant response
jokes.explanation: Homer's illogical comeback that makes no sense but sounds defiant

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Simpson, you're responsible for these avalanches!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Am not! Besides, avalanches are just nature's way of saying hello!

jokes.text: [Gasps] A Bazooka Joe comic. [Groans] I heard that one 75 years ago.
jokes.setup: Homer finds entertainment in desperate situation
jokes.punchline: Burns too old to find any novelty in ancient comic
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Homer's simple pleasures and Burns' jaded age

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mr. Burns, I found this ancient comic book in your office! It's from 1952!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Bah! I was reading that when it came out. It was already old then.

jokes.text: We could build real men out of snow.
jokes.setup: Homer suggests building snowmen
jokes.punchline: Burns suggests more realistic/disturbing alternative
jokes.explanation: Burns escalating innocent activity into something vaguely sinister

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We could build snowmen!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Snowmen? Bah! I have a better idea. We'll build a life-size replica of my house out of snow, then melt it down and use the water to fill my swimming pool.

jokes.text: That moose is on fire! Fine. Good. I don't care anymore.
jokes.setup: Bart reports dangerous situation
jokes.punchline: Smithers completely given up on everything
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Smithers' despair - even wildlife emergency doesn't matter

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Mr. Smithers, the nuclear plant is about to explode!

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Well, aren't you going to do something?

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: What's the point? Mr. Burns didn't say good morning to me today.

jokes.text: 206 bones... 50 miles of small intestine, full, pouting lips. Why, this fellow is less a snowman than a god!
jokes.setup: Homer describing anatomically detailed snowman
jokes.punchline: Elevates craft project to divine status
jokes.explanation: Homer's artistic pride escalating to religious reverence

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I've created a snowman so anatomically detailed and lifelike, it's a work of art. Michelangelo himself would weep at its beauty.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: This isn't just a snowman. This is a masterpiece. This is... this is divine.

jokes.text: Look at them, smug and secure in their finery... mocking us.
jokes.setup: Looking at their dressed snowmen
jokes.punchline: Burns paranoid about snowman attitudes
jokes.explanation: Burns' descent into cabin fever paranoia, attributing malicious intent to snowmen

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Look at those snowmen. They're standing there, watching us.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Plotting. Always plotting.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: We must destroy them before they destroy us.

jokes.text: They're just snowmen, Mr. Burns. Ah, snowmen have peepers. Peepers to watch- to watch for a moment of weakness.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to reassure Burns about snowmen
jokes.punchline: Burns paranoid about snowman surveillance
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of cabin fever paranoia - snowmen as threats

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Don't worry, Mr. Burns. It's just a snowman.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Just a snowman? Homer, snowmen have eyes. They can see everything. They're watching us right now.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mr. Burns, snowmen can't move or talk.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: That's what they want you to think!

jokes.text: Well, I was just happy to see so many nice people.
jokes.setup: Drunk questioned about ranger's whereabouts
jokes.punchline: Cheerful oblivious response to hostility
jokes.explanation: Classic drunk character - happy obliviousness to social situation

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Who, me? I haven't seen him! I've been too busy enjoying this lovely refreshment!

jokes.text: Well, I'll tell you one thing. They didn't come here... for the Mountain Music Festival, March 14-18.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Homer and Burns being trapped
jokes.punchline: Drunk mentions irrelevant music festival
jokes.explanation: Good non-sequitur from drunk character adding irrelevant detail

dialogue.speaker: Drunk
dialogue.line: Yeah, and I heard they're stuck down there with no food or water. At least they got each other... hey, you going to Bonnaroo?

jokes.text: But not in the good Las Vegas way.
jokes.setup: Homer thinks Burns is trying to hypnotize him
jokes.punchline: Clarifies there's a good version of hypnosis
jokes.explanation: Homer's distinction between malicious and entertainment hypnosis

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait a minute. Is Mr. Burns trying to hypnotize me?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm gonna stay alert and fight back with all my will.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, I'm sorry. I didn't mean to imply there's a bad kind of hypnosis.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: There's a good kind of hypnosis too.

jokes.text: Stand back! I have powers! Uh, political powers!
jokes.setup: Burns threatens Homer physically
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he means bureaucratic influence
jokes.explanation: Burns' pathetic attempt to sound threatening with his actual weakness

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: I'm going to destroy you, Simpson. I have the power to ruin your life with a single phone call.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh no! You're gonna kill me?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Kill you? No, no, no. I mean through legal channels and my vast network of influence in city government.

jokes.text: Okay, search party, before we set out... let's take a moment to humor the children. Kids, your father's gonna be just fine.
jokes.setup: Leader organizing search party
jokes.punchline: Condescending reassurance before expecting to find corpses
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about false reassurance before grim task

dialogue.speaker: Search Leader
dialogue.line: Don't worry, we'll find him. These things usually turn out okay.

dialogue.speaker: Search Leader
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, we'll find something.

jokes.text: Okay, search party, before we set out... let's take a moment to humor the children. Kids, your father's gonna be just fine. / Okay, everybody, put on your corpse-handling gloves.
jokes.setup: Reassuring children about their father
jokes.punchline: Immediately preparing for body recovery
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - immediate contradiction between reassurance and preparation for death

dialogue.speaker: Search Leader
dialogue.line: Don't worry, children. We'll find your father.

dialogue.speaker: Search Leader
dialogue.line: Men, get the body bags.

jokes.text: We've got two frozen bodies buried somewhere in this mountain.
jokes.setup: Just told kids father will be fine
jokes.punchline: Immediately discusses finding frozen corpses
jokes.explanation: Immediate contradiction of reassuring words with grim reality

dialogue.speaker: Search leader
dialogue.line: Your father will be fine.

dialogue.speaker: Search leader
dialogue.line: Now, we just need to find those frozen corpses.

jokes.text: Oh, Lord, protect this rocket house and all who dwell within the rocket house.
jokes.setup: Homer and Burns careening down mountain
jokes.punchline: Prayer treating makeshift sled as dwelling
jokes.explanation: Homer's prayer treating their dangerous situation as homesteading

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Dear God, we paid for sushi and you give us fast food!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Shut up, Simpson!

jokes.text: You're fired, Lenny.
jokes.setup: Burns said no one would be fired after learning teamwork
jokes.punchline: Immediately fires someone for arriving last
jokes.explanation: Burns immediately contradicting his own promise in typical fashion

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: No one will be fired.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: You're fired.

jokes.text: You're fired, Lenny. / Aw, nuts!
jokes.setup: Competition resolution, Lenny arrives last
jokes.punchline: Lenny's repeated catchphrase for disappointment
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to Lenny's earlier reaction, consistent character response

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Well, well, well. Last place. How does it feel, Lenny?

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Excellent. In that case, no one will be fired after all.
jokes.setup: Burns asking if they learned about teamwork
jokes.punchline: Completely reversing the stakes after everyone agrees
jokes.explanation: Burns' arbitrary management style - changing rules after the fact based on satisfaction

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Well, I hope you've all learned something about the value of teamwork.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We sure have, Mr. Burns!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Excellent. Then you won't mind working twice as hard for half the pay.

jokes.text: That old goat can't fire me. I'm gonna give him a piece of my- [Gets hit by falling snow]
jokes.setup: Lenny angrily confronting Burns
jokes.punchline: Gets immediately buried by snow
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy timing - anger interrupted by accident

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Mr. Burns, you're a real jerk!

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: I'm gonna tell you exactly what I think of you!

jokes.text: Well, Simpson, I must say... once you've been through something like that with a person... you never want to see that person again. You said it, ya weirdo!
jokes.setup: Burns reflects on bonding experience
jokes.punchline: Both agree they want to avoid each other
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of typical bonding story - mutual rejection after shared trauma

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: That was quite a bonding experience we shared.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, it really brought us closer together.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Indeed. Perhaps we should do it again sometime.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, I don't think that'll be necessary.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: No, I suppose not.

tmdb_overview: To promote teamwork amongst the power plant's employees, Mr. Burns makes them go on a corporate retreat up in the mountains.
insight: Season 8's softest episode: 75 score despite dense 3.5 jokes-per-minute output.
